pairs.question: What is the full title of the 1987 Barbie animated television special?
pairs.answer: The full title of the 1987 Barbie animated television special is 'Barbie and the Rockers: Out of This World'.

pairs.question: Who created Barbie and the Rockers: Out of This World and what was its original release format?
pairs.answer: Barbie and the Rockers: Out of This World was created by DIC Animation City with Saban Productions. It originally aired in syndication as a television special.

pairs.question: Why did Mattel initially avoid media centered around the Barbie character?
pairs.answer: Mattel avoided media centered around Barbie because they were reluctant to give the doll a fixed personality, as they saw Barbie as a 'blank slate' for girls to project their own ideas onto her.

pairs.question: What competition prompted Mattel to begin multimedia expansion with the Barbie character?
pairs.answer: The competition from Hasbro's Jem line, which had a daily cartoon in syndication at the time, prompted Mattel to start expanding the Barbie franchise into multimedia.

pairs.question: What was the intended follow-up to Barbie and the Rockers: Out of This World, and what happened to the plans?
pairs.answer: The intended follow-up was a daily Barbie cartoon series, but negotiations between DIC and Mattel fell through. The project was later reworked into 'Maxie's World' in 1988 with Hasbro's sponsorship.

pairs.question: Provide an example of the media distribution formats used for Barbie and the Rockers: Out of This World after its original airing.
pairs.answer: After its original syndication airing, the special was released as a single VHS tape and, in rare cases, on Betamax by Hi-Tops Video. Foreign rights were handled by Channel 5 Video, a joint venture between Heron Communications and PolyGram.

pairs.question: What unrelated Barbie-related media project was planned for 1987 but never aired?
pairs.answer: A planned live-action/animated hybrid newsmagazine under the Barbie name was intended for 1987, but it never made it to air.
